_meta.status_note: IMPLEMENTED 2026-06-24 under the zero error/debt policy (P0.7)

title: Sub-collection access is not scope-checked (OWASP A01 IDOR) — enforce parent-record scope for list/add across ALL detail panels
business_reason: requireStaffScope enforces only module-level RBAC (can(actor, module, action)); it does not verify that the specific parent id (e.g. a lead) is within the actor's assigned scope. The sub-collection service fns (listInteractions/addInteraction and every other gen_sub-generated pair) take the parent id directly with no scope narrowing, while the parent LIST is scoped via applyListFilters(actor). Result: a region-scoped staff member can read/add child records (CRM interactions, etc.) on a parent outside their assigned scope by passing its id.
owasp: A01 Broken Access Control (IDOR / function-level authorization). Severity: medium (intra-company, gated behind module write permission, but violates the staff-scope policy).
discovered_in: FR-SCHOOL-CRM-2026-0004 (Comms interactions). The hole is in the SHARED sub-collection mechanism (gen_actions.gen_sub), so it affects every module that exposes a detail panel.

implemented_fix.shared_helper: Added recordInScope(actor, table, record, ownerColumn?) to server/security/scope.ts — mirrors the list's AND-of-dimensions `.in` semantics, with an ownership escape hatch (ownerColumn === actor_id) honouring 'assigned_leads OR region'. Global/unscoped staff and non-staff are unrestricted (school scope enforced separately).
implemented_fix.generated_layer: gen_actions.py: (1) the CRM LIST now applies applicableFilters().in() to base + facet queries (it previously applied NO assignment scope — broader gap found during fix); (2) a fail-closed assertLeadInScope() is called by every row action (setStage/assign/lost), the convert chain, and every sub-collection fn (listInteractions/addInteraction/editInteraction). Enforced only when the record loads (DB present), mirroring the kernel — local/dev (no DB) and global actors are unaffected.
covers: interactions list/add/edit (+ all future gen_sub sub-collections)
implemented_fix.errors_status: 422 VALIDATION_FAILED with field 'id' message 'Record not in your scope.' (matches the kernel's existing convention). Reads (list) fail soft to empty; writes fail closed.
implemented_fix.tests: tests/unit/crm_scope.test.ts — recordInScope unit cases + service-level A01 enforcement (add/edit/setStage reject out-of-scope; list returns empty; in-scope and global pass). Full suite 102/102, tsc clean.
implemented_fix.kernel_also_fixed: Per the zero error/debt policy (P0.7), the same A01 class in the generic kernel was closed in the same pass: defineModule.ownsRecord now also enforces recordInScope for staff-scoped modules (was school-actor-only), so every defineModule-based module gets the fail-closed per-record check too — not just CRM.

security_owasp: A01: scoped staff adding/reading an interaction on an out-of-scope lead -> FORBIDDEN
A01: in-scope lead still works (no false-deny)
A07: assertion is fail-closed when the parent row cannot be loaded
regression: existing in-scope comms add/list from FR-0004 still pass
